page: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 4 vCPUs. This CPU configuration is paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 100 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps along with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the b3-16 delivers strong single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by its stress-ng single-core score and text processing capabilities, both of which rank in the top 25% of tested servers. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and software compilation speeds fall within the average range. The server also achieves average marks in database operations, Redis SET operations, and small-model LLM inference. However, static web serving throughput and prompt processing for medium-sized 7B LLM models are weaker, placing in the bottom 25% of tested configurations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores and integrated NVMe storage provide a reliable resource density for general-purpose workloads without the overhead of multi-threading. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance suitable for application hosting, text processing, software development environments, and moderate database operations. It is less suited for high-throughput static web serving or intensive large-scale language model inference due to its lower network baseline and average multi-core scaling.
description: The OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC Milan x86_64 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.3 GHz with 1 thread per core, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 100 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is established at a baseline of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain average. Conversely, static web serving throughput is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%. This resource profile offers balanced cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads, making it suitable for application hosting, software compilation, and moderate database tasks, though it is less optimal for high-throughput web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for balanced workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 16 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores and NVMe storage for reliable single-core performance.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores at 2.3 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suited for application hosting, software compilation, and database tasks
